title: Z-score
short: How many standard deviations a value lies from the mean: z = (x − μ) / σ.
full:
A z-score (standard score) rescales a value by stating how many standard deviations it sits above or below the mean: z = (x − μ) / σ. A positive z is above the mean, a negative z below.

Converting to z-scores 'standardizes' any normal distribution to the standard normal (μ = 0, σ = 1), so a single table of probabilities works for all of them. For example a z of 1.5 corresponds to a cumulative probability of about 0.9332.
